I watched the 36-minute 21-second version released by the sponsor 'Positive Short Movie Sponsorship by Sherring Ng' on YouTube, and the 34-minute 53-second version streamed online during the 3rd Hong Kong Onscreen Film Festival. I was more attached to the slightly darker and heavier former version, but also more impressed by the ending of the later version: the roadside lights and the Old Supreme Court Building lit up remind me of lyrics from the song 'Promise 2020' by Albert Leung Wai Man, 'still recall the streetlights illuminating the yellow thoughts' and 'have not forgotten the promise to meet again at the rice cooker'.

This is a diary of an outsider, not a Hong Konger, which is fine in itself. But most of what the documentary presents applies to outsiders of other big cities as well. In other words, I am interested in an outsider in Hong Kong, not an outsider in a big city which happens to be Hong Kong.
